snp4.2:1.1: Staying attached to the cave, covered heavily over,
snp4.2:1.4: for sensual pleasures sensual desires in the world are not lightly let go.
snp4.2:2.1: Those chained by desire, bound by becoming’s allure,
snp4.2:2.2: aren’t easily released for there’s no liberation by others.
snp4.2:2.4: on hunger for sensual pleasures here or before—
snp4.2:3.1: greedy for sensual pleasures, busy, deluded, ungenerous,
snp4.2:3.4: “What will we be when we pass on from here?”
snp4.2:4.1: So a person should train right here-&-now.
snp4.2:4.2: Whatever you know as discordant in the world,
snp4.2:4.3: don’t, for its sake, act discordantly,
snp4.2:4.4: for that life, the enlightened say, is short.
snp4.2:5.1: I see them, in the world, floundering around,
snp4.2:5.2: people immersed in craving for states of becoming.
snp4.2:5.3: Base people moan in the mouth of death,
snp4.2:5.4: their craving, for states of becoming & not-, unallayed.
snp4.2:6.1: See them, floundering in their sense of mine,
snp4.2:6.2: like fish in the puddles of a dried-up stream—
snp4.2:6.3: and, seeing this, live with no mine,
snp4.2:6.4: not forming attachment for states of becoming.
snp4.2:7.2: comprehending sensory contact, with no greed.
snp4.2:7.3: Doing nothing for which he himself would rebuke himself,
snp4.2:7.4: the enlightened person doesn’t adhere to what’s seen, to what’s heard.
snp4.2:8.1: Comprehending perception, he’d cross over the flood—
snp4.2:8.2: the sage not stuck on possessions.
snp4.2:8.4: living heedfully, he longs for neither— this world, the next.
